Fit a Bayesian Hierarchical Regression Model and Summarize Posterior Metrics

Source: R/utils-fit_bayesian_hierarchical_model.R
fit_bayesian_hierarchical_model.Rd

This function fits a Bayesian hierarchical regression model using the brms package and computes posterior summary statistics for signal-level metrics. The model captures signal clustering within themes and provides detailed posterior metrics for use in performance analysis.

Usage

fit_bayesian_hierarchical_model(
  signal_universe_m_d_ref,
  selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref,
  selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref,
  selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_upd_ref = NULL,
  elected_priors,
  selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref,
  model_spec_theme_level,
  chains,
  iter,
  warmup,
  thin,
  seed,
  adapt_delta,
  verbose,
  parallel
)

Arguments

signal_universe_m_d_ref

A data frame containing the signal universe. If provided, data in this object will be updated with posteriors.

selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref

A xts containing the backtest returns data for various signals.

selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref

A numeric vector containing benchmark returns data. The vector will be recycled to match the length of the backtest returns data.

selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_upd_ref

An already processed selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref. This data.frame is already in long format and contemplates both the selected_market_factor_proxy_m_xts_upd_ref and the selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref theme data.

elected_priors

An object of class brmsprior containing priors for the Bayesian model, such as those generated by the derive_informative_priors_from_data function or provided directly by the user.

selected_signal_themes_m_d_ref

A data frame containing metadata about signals. This data frame should include:

  • tickers: Signal identifiers matching those in selected_backtest_returns_corrected_positions_m_xts_upd_ref.

  • theme: Group membership for each signal, defining the clusters for the Bayesian hierarchical model.

  • dates: Dates corresponding to the backtest data. This input ensures proper alignment between signals and their associated themes.

model_spec_theme_level

A character string specifying the desired Bayesian model structure. Options include:

  • "random_intercept_fixed_slope": Includes random effects for the intercept at the theme level.

  • "theme_specific_intercept_fixed_slope": Uses fixed intercepts for each theme.

  • "theme_specific_intercept_theme_specific_slope": Includes fixed intercepts and slopes for each theme.

  • "fixed_intercept_fixed_slope": Omits theme-level intercepts but includes random effects at the theme:signal level.

chains

An integer specifying the number of Markov chains to run. Defaults to 4.

iter

An integer specifying the number of iterations per chain. Defaults to 2000.

warmup

An integer specifying the number of warmup iterations. Defaults to 1000.

thin

An integer specifying the thinning interval. Defaults to 1.

seed

An integer, or NA, specifying the random seed for reproducibility. No default; passed through from the caller.

adapt_delta

A numeric value between 0 and 1 specifying the target acceptance rate for the Hamiltonian Monte Carlo sampler.

verbose

A logical indicating whether to display progress messages during the model fitting process. Defaults to TRUE.

parallel

A logical indicating whether to enable parallel computation using future. Defaults to TRUE.

Value

A named list containing:

  • brm_model: A brmsfit object containing the fitted Bayesian model. This object includes posterior distributions, parameter estimates, diagnostics, and other details of the model fit.

  • signal_universe_m_d_ref: A data frame containing the updated signal_universe_m_d_ref with posterior summary statistics, or NULL if signal_universe_m_d_ref was NULL.

  • posterior_draws_summaries: A list of median/89\ (see summarize_posteriors_draws()), or NULL if signal_universe_m_d_ref was NULL.

Details

The function performs the following steps:

  1. Preprocessing: Converts the backtest returns data into long format, merges metadata about signals and themes, and adds the market factor proxy.

  2. Formula Specification: Defines the Bayesian model formula based on the chosen model_spec_theme_level.

  3. Model Fitting: Fits the Bayesian model using the brm function from the brms package, incorporating the provided priors and prepared data.

  4. Posterior Summarization: Extracts posterior draws and computes signal-level metrics such as alphas, betas, sigmas, active returns, tracking errors, information ratios (IR), appraisal ratios (AP), and Treynor ratios. Updates the signal_universe_m_d_ref data frame with these metrics.

This hierarchical approach allows the model to capture both global (theme-level) and local (signal-level) effects while leveraging the flexibility of Bayesian regression. The inclusion of posterior metrics enables deeper performance analysis of signals.

On this page

Developed by Paulo Guimaraes.
